---
title: "nitrix.geometry.affine"
generated: true
signature-hash: bc549a6dee62f227
description: "Geometric-parameter affine algebra (batched, differentiable)."
listing:
  id: nitrix-geometry-affine-symbols
  contents: "nitrix-geometry-affine/*.qmd"
  type: table
  fields: [title, description]
  sort: "title"
  filter-ui: false
  page-size: 500
---

::: {.callout-important title="Generated reference stub — fill me"}
Auto-generated scaffold for `nitrix.geometry.affine`. Fill the prose *outside* the
generated markers; do **not** ship as-is (SPEC §6.5). `check_reference_staleness.py` flags signature drift.
:::

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:__module__ -->
Geometric-parameter affine algebra (batched, differentiable).

The complement to the Lie-group chart in [`nitrix.geometry.transform`](nitrix-geometry-transform.qmd)
([`rigid_exp`](nitrix-geometry-transform/rigid_exp.qmd) / [`affine_exp`](nitrix-geometry-transform/affine_exp.qmd)): here an affine is parametrised by
its geometric factors -- translation, Euler rotation, anisotropic scale,
and shear -- and composed as $T\,R\,S\,E$.  This is the
parametrisation used when those factors are individually meaningful (for
example, reading off the scales of a fitted transform, or constraining a
fit to be rigid by dropping scale and shear), as opposed to the exponential
chart, which is the natural one for unconstrained gradient-based
optimisation.

Also here: [`fit_affine`](nitrix-geometry-affine/fit_affine.qmd), the closed-form (weighted) least-squares
affine between two corresponding point sets.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen sections:__module__ -->
::: {.callout-note}
Conventions:

- Matrices are $(\ldots, N, N + 1)$ (the implied last row is
  $(0, \ldots, 0, 1)$) unless made square; everything is batched over
  leading dimensions and differentiable.  A transform maps coordinates as
  $y = \mathtt{mat}[\ldots, :, :-1]\,x + \mathtt{mat}[\ldots, :, -1]$.
- Rotations: 2-D is a single planar angle; 3-D is right-handed
  *intrinsic* $R = X\,Y\,Z$ (rotate about x, then y, then z); angles
  in degrees by default.  The left-to-right axis order reads the same as the
  matrix product because intrinsic rotations post-multiply: $X\,Y\,Z$
  is equivalently the *extrinsic* sequence z-then-y-then-x (rightmost factor
  applied first), the same rightmost-applied-first convention as the
  $T\,R\,S\,E$ factor composition above.  (Intrinsic rotations are
  about the object axes, which move with the object; extrinsic rotations are
  about the fixed world axes.)
- The rotation / parameter chart supports `ndim` in `{2, 3}` (the Euler
  convention is dimension-specific, but both are covered);
  [`params_to_affine_matrix`](nitrix-geometry-affine/params_to_affine_matrix.qmd) takes an explicit `ndim` because a
  6-vector is ambiguous (rigid 3-D vs full 2-D), while
  [`affine_matrix_to_params`](nitrix-geometry-affine/affine_matrix_to_params.qmd) infers it from the matrix shape.  The
  shape helpers and [`fit_affine`](nitrix-geometry-affine/fit_affine.qmd) are N-D.

On GPU, the factorisations (inverse / Cholesky / determinant) are routed
through the library's safe solver wrappers (or computed analytically for
the 3x3 / diagonal cases) so they work on the cuSolver-affected GPU stacks,
where the dense-solver handle pool is broken.
:::
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen dims:__page__ -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
| `n` | query points, each an `ndim`-vector coordinate (variadic) |
| `np1` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `m` | rows of a multi-row contrast |
| `a` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `d` | spatial dimension (2 or 3) |
| `p` | fixed-effect coefficients / design-matrix columns |
| `d1` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## Symbols

::: {#nitrix-geometry-affine-symbols}
:::
